zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Somerset於1274年7月25日出生在英國薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘時，他的父親John de Beauchamp11271歲，母親Cecily de Vivonne17歲。他於1300年之前與Joan Chenduit在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1337年1月1日在英國英格蘭享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特哈姆頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Somerset于1274年7月25日出生在英国薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘时，他的父亲John de Beauchamp11271岁，母亲Cecily de Vivonne17岁。他于1300年之前与Joan Chenduit在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1337年1月1日在英国英格兰享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰薩默塞特哈姆頓。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Somerset was born on 25 July 1274, in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, his father, John de Beauchamp, was 11271 and his mother, Cecily de Vivonne, was 17. He married Joan Chenduit before 1300, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 1 January 1337, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in Hamdon, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Somerset est né le 25 juillet 1274, à Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, John de Beauchamp, avait 11271 ans et sa mère, Cecily de Vivonne, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Joan Chenduit avant 1300, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 1 janvier 1337, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Hamdon, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England: Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Somerset am 25. Juli 1274 in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Beauchamp 11271 und seine Mutter Cecily de Vivonne 17. Er hat vor 1300 in England, United Kingdom Joan Chenduit geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 1. Januar 1337 im Alter von 62 Jahren in England, United Kingdom und wurde in Hamdon, Somerset, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Somerset è nato il il 25 luglio 1274 nel Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, il suo padre, John de Beauchamp, aveva 11271 anni e il suo madre, Cecily de Vivonne, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Joan Chenduit prima del 1300, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 1 gennaio 1337, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Hamdon, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Somersetが1274年7月25日にイギリスソメルセトハトチ ビーウチャンップで生まれた時，父John de Beauchampは11271歳，母Cecily de Vivonneは17歳であった。1300年以前にイギリスイギリスでJoan Chenduitと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1337年1月1日にイギリスイギリスで62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトハムドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Somerset(이)가 1274년 7월 25일 영국 서머세트 해트치 보챔프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Beauchamp(은)는 11271세였고, 그의 어머니 Cecily de Vivonne(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1300년 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 Joan Chenduit(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1337년 1월 1일 영국 잉글랜드에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 함던에 묻혔다.

pt.Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England: Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Somerset nasceu em 25 de julho de 1274, em Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Beauchamp, tinha 11271 anos e sua mãe, Cecily de Vivonne, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Chenduit antes 1300, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1 de janeiro de 1337, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Hamdon, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Somerset родился 25 июля 1274 г. в местности хач бочамп, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени John de Beauchamp было 11271 год, а матери, которую звали Cecily de Vivonne, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Chenduit перед 1300 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 1 января 1337 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности хамдон, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Somerset nació el 25 de julio de 1274, en Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Beauchamp, tenía 11271 años y su mamá, Cecily de Vivonne, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Joan Chenduit antes de 1300, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 1 de enero de 1337, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Hamdon, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
